upcaster_notes: CreditAnalysisCompleted v2 events with null denormalized top-level fields (confidence_score, risk_tier, recommended_limit_usd) require UpcasterRegistry entry — see contracts/upcasters.py (read-time upcasting, not one-shot migration).

deviations.canonical_type: event_id, aggregate_id, aggregate_type, sequence_number, metadata{}, schema_version, occurred_at, recorded_at
deviations.note: stream_id dropped from migrated top-level (recover from source). event_id deterministic UUID5 per line identity. aggregate_id UUID5(application_id) or stream_id fallback.
correlation_id = UUID5(namespace, session_id or stream_id). causation_id and user_id null. source_service constant documented in script.
schema_version set to "1.0"; event_version not part of canonical envelope — see upcaster note.

deviations.canonical_type: number for USD amounts where schema expects number
deviations.actual_type: string digits for requested_amount_usd, approved_amount_usd, decision.recommended_limit_usd
event_version 2 with confidence_score, risk_tier, recommended_limit_usd null at payload top; values live under decision{}
deviations.note: requires UpcasterRegistry entry — see contracts/upcasters.py (read-time); migration does not fabricate denormalized fields.
User-reported field absent from outputs/week5/events.jsonl; no strip migration applied.

deviations.canonical_type: ISO8601 with consistent offset or Z
deviations.actual_type: recorded_at uses +00:00; many payload *at fields lack timezone
deviations.note: recorded_at normalized to Z. occurred_at uses first timezone-aware payload timestamp, else falls back to recorded_at with documented approximation.
Assigned after global sort by (recorded_at, source line index) within each aggregate bucket.
